## Zhongji Innolight Seeks Up to $7B Hong Kong Listing Backed by BlackRock, Hillhouse, and Temasek

A well-funded, tier-1-backed Innolight accelerates the competitive threat to COHR and LITE in AI datacenter optical interconnects; margin and share assumptions for Western transceiver players may need revisiting if Innolight uses IPO capital to cut price or expand reach outside China.

- Chinese optical transceiver maker Zhongji Innolight is seeking up to $7.02B in a Hong Kong IPO.
- Anchor backing from BlackRock, Hillhouse, and Temasek lends the listing institutional credibility.
- Innolight is a direct competitor to COHR and LITE in the datacom transceiver market.
- IPO proceeds would materially expand Innolight's capacity to compete in AI networking components.
